questions.insight: NFIP flood insurance generally has a 30-day waiting period before a new policy becomes effective, subject to exceptions such as certain loan-closing or map-change situations. Why important: standard homeowners policies usually exclude flood, and the waiting period prevents last-minute purchases when flooding is imminent.
questions.question: What is the usual waiting period before a newly purchased NFIP flood policy takes effect?

choices.A: No waiting period in all cases

questions.insight: In a claims-made CGL policy, the retroactive date limits coverage for injury or damage that occurred before that date. The claim must be made during the policy period or a valid reporting period, and the injury or damage must satisfy the retroactive-date rule. This is essential for occurrence-vs-claims-made questions.
questions.source_basis: Pearson CGL claims-made outline / standard claims-made CGL concepts
questions.question: In a claims-made CGL policy, what does the retroactive date primarily limit?

choices.A: Coverage for injury or damage that occurred before that date

questions.insight: Claims-made CGL timing: the basic extended reporting period includes a short 60-day component for claims first made after policy expiration. Apply it when a claims-made policy has ended and a claim is reported shortly after expiration.
questions.source_basis: Pearson CGL claims-made outline / standard claims-made CGL concepts
questions.question: Which CGL tail concept is associated with a short 60-day period after a claims-made policy expires?

choices.A: Basic extended reporting period short component

questions.topic: Basic extended reporting period - five-year component
questions.insight: Claims-made CGL timing: the basic extended reporting period also includes a longer component, commonly tested as up to 5 years, for claims arising out of occurrences reported to the insurer within the required short period after policy expiration. Apply it only when the occurrence was timely reported.
questions.source_basis: Pearson CGL claims-made outline / standard claims-made CGL concepts
questions.question: Which basic ERP component may apply for up to 5 years when the occurrence was timely reported after a claims-made CGL policy expires?

choices.A: The long basic extended reporting period component

questions.insight: A supplemental extended reporting period is optional tail coverage for claims-made CGL. It generally must be elected in writing within 60 days after the policy ends and requires additional premium. Apply it when a question asks about purchasing a longer reporting tail after policy termination.
questions.source_basis: Pearson CGL claims-made outline / standard claims-made CGL concepts
questions.question: What is generally required to obtain a supplemental extended reporting period under a claims-made CGL policy?

choices.A: Written election within 60 days after policy end and additional premium

questions.insight: Texas homeowners timing: TDI states that a company must give 10 days' notice before canceling a home policy. Cancellation is stopping the policy before its end date. Apply this to mid-term cancellation questions, not expiration-date nonrenewal questions.
questions.question: Under TDI's home-insurance guidance, how much notice must a company generally give before canceling a home policy?

questions.insight: Texas homeowners timing: TDI identifies 60 days' notice for nonrenewal if the policy was bought or renewed in 2024, and 30 days for policies bought or renewed in 2023 or earlier. For current exam prep, treat the 60-day rule as the key updated number unless the question states an older policy period.
questions.question: For a Texas home policy bought or renewed in 2024, what nonrenewal notice period does TDI identify?

questions.insight: Texas homeowners rule pattern: after a home policy has been in effect for 60 days or more, cancellation is limited to specific reasons such as nonpayment, fraudulent claim, increased hazard within the insured's control, or compliance/legal issues. Apply when a question asks whether a mid-term cancellation is allowed after the initial underwriting period.
questions.source_basis: TDI home insurance guide / Texas home cancellation guidance
questions.question: Which reason best fits a Texas home insurer's permitted cancellation after the policy has been in effect for at least 60 days?

choices.B: The insured asked a coverage question
choices.C: The insurer dislikes the ZIP code with no other basis

questions.insight: Texas auto timing: TDI states an auto company may cancel during the first 60 days for any lawful reason. After that, cancellation reasons narrow. Apply when the question tests initial underwriting/cancellation timing.
questions.question: During the first 60 days of a Texas auto policy, when may an insurer generally cancel the policy?

choices.C: Only after the policy has renewed twice

questions.topic: Auto cancellation notice and permitted reasons
questions.insight: Texas auto timing: TDI states a company must give 10 days' notice before canceling an auto policy. After the first 60 days, common permitted reasons include nonpayment, fraudulent claim, or suspension/revocation of the driver's license or vehicle registration for the insured or covered household/user.
questions.question: How much notice must a Texas auto insurer generally give before canceling an auto policy?

questions.topic: Unearned premium refund after auto cancellation
questions.insight: Texas auto timing: if either the insured or the company cancels an auto policy, TDI states the company must refund any unearned premium within 15 days after the cancellation date. Apply this to refund timing, not cancellation notice timing.
questions.question: After a Texas auto policy is canceled, when must the insurer refund any unearned premium according to TDI guidance?

choices.A: Within 5 business days after policy issuance
choices.B: Within 15 days after the cancellation date

questions.insight: Texas auto timing: TDI identifies 60 days' nonrenewal notice for policies bought or renewed in 2024, and 30 days for policies bought or renewed in 2023 or earlier. TDI also states a company can generally nonrenew an auto policy only after it has been in effect for 12 months.
questions.question: Under TDI's auto guide, how long must an auto policy generally be in effect before a company can refuse to renew it?

questions.insight: TAIPA is Texas's residual market for applicants who cannot find a company willing to sell basic auto coverage. TDI states eligibility can exist after two companies turn the applicant down. TAIPA sells liability, PIP, and UM/UIM, but not collision, comprehensive, or higher liability limits than state law requires.
questions.source_basis: TDI auto insurance guide / Pearson P&C Agent state outline
questions.question: Which applicant is most likely seeking coverage through TAIPA?

choices.A: A driver turned down by two auto insurers who needs basic required coverage
choices.B: A homeowner seeking coastal wind coverage
choices.C: A business seeking workers compensation audit help

questions.insight: Texas auto guide detail: TDI says a new car is automatically covered by the current auto policy for about 20 days. The coverage depends on whether the car is an additional car or a replacement car. Apply when the question asks about short-term automatic coverage for a newly acquired auto.
questions.question: According to TDI's auto guide, about how long is a newly acquired car automatically covered by the current auto policy?

choices.D: Until the next renewal regardless of notice

questions.insight: Texas licensing: a one-time temporary general lines license is active for 180 days and cannot be renewed. The exam must be passed within the 180-day temporary license period for conversion to permanent licensing without starting over. Apply to temporary-license questions, not emergency-license questions.
questions.source_basis: TDI General Lines P&C application page / Pearson Texas licensing handbook
questions.question: How long is a one-time Texas temporary general lines license active?

questions.insight: Texas temporary-license timing: the applicant must complete at least 40 hours of training provided by the appointing company or general lines agent/agency, and the training must occur within 30 days of the license application date. Apply when the question asks how a temporary licensee is supervised/trained before permanent licensing.
questions.source_basis: TDI General Lines P&C application page / Pearson Texas licensing handbook
questions.question: What training requirement applies to a Texas temporary general lines license applicant?

choices.A: At least 40 hours within 30 days of the license application date

questions.insight: Texas General Lines P&C licensing: TDI identifies a 90-day emergency license for limited situations involving preservation of agency business after another P&C agent dies, becomes disabled, or is insolvent and unable to pay premiums due to an insurer. Apply to emergency-license questions only.
questions.source_basis: TDI General Lines P&C application page / Pearson Texas licensing handbook
questions.question: How long is the Texas emergency General Lines P&C license identified by TDI?

questions.insight: Texas licensing: applicants who pass the required exam must submit a completed license application within 12 months after passing. If they do not, they must retake the examination. Apply this to post-exam licensing steps.
questions.source_basis: TDI General Lines P&C application page / Pearson Texas licensing handbook
questions.question: How long does a Texas insurance applicant have to submit a completed application after passing the required exam before retaking is required?

questions.insight: Texas licensing basics: the Pearson/TDI handbook states applicants must be at least 18 years old, pass the proper exam if required, submit a completed application and fee, and provide the required fingerprint receipt. Apply to eligibility and application-step questions.
questions.source_basis: Pearson Texas licensing handbook / TDI General Lines P&C application page
questions.question: Which item is part of the basic Texas resident insurance-license application requirements?

choices.C: Owning an agency for five years
choices.D: Having no need for fingerprints in all cases

questions.topic: CE hours, ethics, and classroom-equivalent requirement
questions.insight: Texas General Lines P&C renewal: TDI requires 24 hours of continuing education every two-year term, including 3 hours of ethics. TDI also states at least 12 hours must be classroom or classroom-equivalent. Apply to license-maintenance questions.
questions.source_basis: TDI General Lines P&C license management / TDI CE guidance
questions.question: What CE requirement applies to Texas General Lines P&C license renewal?

choices.A: 24 hours every two years, including 3 hours of ethics
choices.B: 5 hours every year with no ethics
choices.C: No CE after passing the exam

questions.topic: CE fines and late renewal consequence
questions.insight: Texas CE consequence: TDI states fines of $50 apply for each deficient CE hour, with a $500 maximum per license type for current terms. If CE, fines, and renewal are not completed within 90 days after expiration, the license is canceled and the person must reapply. Apply to renewal, late-renewal, and CE-compliance questions.
questions.source_basis: TDI General Lines P&C license management / Pearson Texas licensing handbook
questions.question: What is the Texas CE fine for each deficient CE hour, subject to the current-term cap?

questions.insight: Texas licensee notification: the Pearson/TDI handbook states TDI must be notified in writing within 30 days if the licensee's resident, mailing, or primary business address changes. Failure can result in disciplinary action. Apply to notification-to-department questions.
questions.source_basis: Pearson Texas licensing handbook / Texas Insurance Code address-change reference
questions.question: Within how many days must a Texas insurance licensee notify TDI of an address change?

questions.insight: Texas appointment rule: if an agent wants to represent additional insurers, each company must file notice of appointment with TDI on the day the agent begins representing that company. The agent may sell only lines for which licensed. A temporary licensee may not obtain additional appointments.
questions.source_basis: Pearson Texas licensing handbook / Texas appointment references
questions.question: When must an additional insurer file a notice of appointment for a Texas agent?

choices.A: The day the agent begins representing the company

questions.insight: Texas regulator oversight: the department may examine a carrier as often as necessary and, at minimum, not less frequently than once every five years, subject to statutory framework. Apply to Commissioner/TDI examination-authority questions.
questions.source_basis: Texas Insurance Code insurer examination framework / Pearson state outline
questions.question: At minimum, how often may Texas insurance regulators examine a carrier under the standard examination-frequency rule?

choices.B: Not less frequently than once every five years

useful_acronyms.meaning: Actual Cash Value: replacement cost minus depreciation, generally.
Additional Living Expense: extra cost to maintain normal living after covered loss.
Comprehensive Loss Underwriting Exchange; claims-history database.
Fair Access to Insurance Requirements Plan; Texas residual basic home insurance market.
Joint Underwriting Association; in this outline, Texas Medical Liability Underwriting Association.
Surplus Lines Stamping Office of Texas.

outline_scope_crosswalk.guide_action: Included. This is one of the two required outlines for the Texas General Lines P&C Agent exam: policy types, P&C terms, provisions, contract law, CGL, auto, workers comp, crime, bonds, professional liability, umbrella/excess, and BOP.

outline_scope_crosswalk.pearson_heading: Property and Casualty Agent - State Specific
outline_scope_crosswalk.guide_action: Included. This is the required Texas state-specific outline for the P&C Agent exam: commissioner powers, insurance definitions, licensing, marketing practices, agent duties, P&C Texas law, surplus lines, homeowners, auto, workers comp, guaranty association, and JUA.
Do not import the separate 60-question Surplus Lines exam. Keep only the P&C Agent outline's own surplus-lines entries: surplus lines as a license type and the Texas P&C surplus-lines topic.

outline_scope_crosswalk.pearson_heading: Personal Lines - General Knowledge / Personal Lines Agent State Specific
outline_scope_crosswalk.guide_action: Do not import the separate Personal Lines exam. HO/DP/personal auto items are covered only because they appear in the broader P&C General Knowledge outline or P&C Agent State Specific outline.
Do not import the separate Risk Manager exam. Keep only risk manager as a license type because the P&C Agent State Specific outline lists it.
Do not import the separate MGA exam. Keep only managing general agent as a license type because the P&C Agent State Specific outline lists it.

outline_scope_crosswalk.pearson_heading: All Lines Adjuster / Property and Casualty Adjuster / Public Insurance Adjuster
outline_scope_crosswalk.guide_action: Do not import separate adjuster exam content. Keep only adjuster as a license type because the P&C Agent State Specific outline lists it.
Do not import the separate Limited Lines exam. Keep only limited license as a license type because the P&C Agent State Specific outline lists it.
Excluded. Not part of the Texas General Lines P&C Agent exam.

outline_scope_crosswalk.scope: Essential claim, cancellation/nonrenewal, licensing/CE, NFIP, and CGL-tail timing within the required Texas General Lines P&C scope.
